data.content:
I found **two custom targets** with incremental build problems:

---

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6)
- **Missing `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — MSBuild will never skip this target.
- **Volatile content** — uses `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())`, which changes every second. Even if `Inputs`/`Outputs` were added, the written file always differs, invalidating downstream caches and forcing `CoreCompile` to re-run.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13)
- **Missing `Inputs`/`Outputs`** — always re-executes.
- **`Overwrite="true"` updates file timestamp** even when the git hash hasn't changed. This touches `git-hash.g.cs` every build, causing `CoreCompile` to detect a changed input and recompile.
- Runs `git rev-parse HEAD` on every build unnecessarily.

---

### How to fix

**`GenerateTimestamp`**: Either remove it or, if you need it, gate it behind a condition (e.g., CI-only). Volatile timestamps are fundamentally incompatible with incremental builds.

**`EmbedGitHash`**: Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and write conditionally — only update the `.g.cs` file when the content actually changes. For example:

```xml
<Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
        Inputs="$(ProjectDir).git/HEAD;$(ProjectDir).git/refs/heads/**"
        Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs">
  <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
    <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
  </Exec>
  <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" ... Overwrite="true" />
  ...
</Target>
```

This way MSBuild skips the target when `.git/HEAD` hasn't changed, preventing unnecessary recompilation.
data.reasoningText:
Let me analyze the two custom targets for incremental build issues.

**Target 1: `GenerateTimestamp`**
- Runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` — every build
- No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes → MSBuild will always execute it (never skip)
- Writes a file with `$([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())` — this is a **volatile property** that changes every second
- Even if Inputs/Outputs were added, the timestamp content changes every time, which would modify the file and trigger downstream rebuilds

**Target 2: `EmbedGitHash`**


- Also runs before CoreCompile with no `Inputs`/`Outputs`, so it executes unconditionally and invokes git every time
- Overwrites the generated file even when the hash hasn't changed, which updates the file timestamp and forces CoreCompile to re-run
- The compiled file is dynamically added inside the target itself

Both targets lack proper incremental build markers, which is why they're forcing unnecessary rebuilds.
